reasoning_raw_text:
QQQ data package is sparse on historical context. I have only current price ($711.44), previous close ($707.08), and volume (0.81x average). No moving averages, no streak data, no range position, no distance from 52-week high.

Raw facts: +0.62% move, below-average volume. This is a modest up move on thin tape — textbook noise signature, not conviction.

Macro regime is neutral. Fed paused, rates stable, VIX normal. No immediate sector compression. However, QQQ holds NVDA, AVGO, TSM as top components — all exposed to geopolitical tail risk (export controls, Taiwan Strait). The Singapore chip smuggling probe and China's AI ambitions (noted in intelligence feed) create ambient risk that is not yet priced.

Chain context shows SMH +2.97% vs QQQ +0.62% — hardware outperforming software/platform. This is a regime signal worth noting: semiconductor strength is outpacing Nasdaq-100 breadth. This divergence could indicate either (a) a temporary hardware rotation or (b) early signs that QQQ's software layer is losing relative momentum.

Without historical data, I cannot compute a high-confidence direction. The volume signature (below average) and the modest move (+0.62%) suggest the most probable outcome is consolidation or pullback, not continuation. HOLD is the appropriate stance — neither accumulate (volume not there) nor exit (no sell signal yet).

Confidence is 2/5 because the data package is incomplete. With only one session snapshot and no historical context, any directional call is statistically weak.
